texts.full_text: in terms of their growth rates. When someone says "Big-Oh notation does not have a total order," it means there are pairs of functions for which neither function is asymptotically bounded by the other using just Big-Oh. To put this into perspective, consider what you've already mentioned about \(\Theta\)-notation (theta notation). If \(f(n) = \Theta(g(n))\) and vice versa (\(g(n) = \Theta(f(n)))\), then both functions grow at the same rate asymptotically. This implies a total order because for any two functions, you can always determine whether one is in Big-Oh of another or not (and if so, also that it's Theta). However, with \(O\) notation alone: 1. If we have two distinct functions \(f(n)\) and \(g(n)\), where neither function grows at the same rate as the other but rather each has a different growth pattern: - It is possible to find cases where you cannot say that either one of them dominates the other in terms of Big-Oh. 2. For example, consider two functions: \[ f(n) = n \] and \[ g(n) = n^{1 + \sin(\log_3\sqrt{n})}. \] - Here \(g\) oscillates between being slightly less than or greater than \(n\) as it includes a sinusoidal term that periodically changes the exponent. So I have to "navigate blindly" around the interface, until I can relaunch. This occurs throughout the operating system. Screenshots: This is what happened when I tried opening the File menu in Lightroom app. What happened to me on Lynda.com (in Firefox) after selecting the "Software..." dropdown. I asked, *"Une baguette, s’il vous plaît, mais… pas trop croustillante?"* (A baguette, please, but… not too crispy?). The baker blinked, then laughed so hard he nearly dropped a croissant on my head. “ means , you dolt!” he said, before handing me the baguette with a wink. See, this is the magic of language: even when you botch it, respect and humor keep you in the good graces. I learned fast that a or can smooth over any grammatical chaos. It’s the same with presentations. You don’t need to nail every word to win an audience—just acknowledge their effort to listen, and reward them with a smile, a pause, or a genuine laugh when you stumble. Tip for the week? texts.full_text: increased weight due to unnecessary usage of concrete (i.e., compared to the cavity/rib type wall section). There are substantial difficulties in producing a variable length, cavity/rib type wall section, since if the desired length causes a side end of the wall to fall in the middle of a cavity, such side end will have an insufficient wall thickness, making it structurally unusable. There is therefore a need to provide an improved precast concrete wall section, and a system for making the same, that minimizes or eliminates one or more of the shortcomings as set forth above. The present invention reduces or eliminates large inventories of precast concrete wall sections having a wide variety of dimensions and provides a variable length, rib cavity type wall section. In accordance with the present invention, an adjustable casting tool is provided for forming various-sized concrete wall sections. The casting tool includes a base, one or more pan members, a pair of opposing sidewalls, and first and second opposing bulkheads. The base is configured for orientation in a horizontal plane and has an upper surface. The base further includes a plurality of receiving locations spaced apart along a first axis. Pan members are attached at selected receiving locations. The remaining space on the base unoccupied by pan members defines a remainder length, which is taken along the first axis. texts.full_text: histology and a usually rapid response to corticosteroids, as well as some other distinguishing factors highlight the uniqueness of this condition compared to systemic sclerosis (sclerodermatous disorders). Shulman's syndrome is primarily characterized by its distinctive clinical presentation which includes skin thickening that affects specific areas such as limbs and abdomen withoutinvolvement typical in sclerosingconditions, like Raynaud’s phenomenon. The absence or minimal presence of visceral symptoms further differentiates it from other connective tissue diseases. The laboratory findings are significant for hypereosinophilia(an elevated eosophil count) which is a hallmark feature not commonly seenwith classic systemic sclerosis but aligns well within the diagnostic criteria proposed by Shulman and colleagues. Additionally, hypergammaglobulinemia suggests an ongoing immune response or chronic inflammation in affected individuals. Histologically speaking, fasciitis characterized as partof this syndrome involves infiltration primarily around connective tissue components such that there is notable eosinophilic involvement which differentiates it from the fibrosis seen with scleroderma. The presence and role of mast cells within biopsy samples also suggest a unique inflammatory process at playin Shulman's. The therapeutic responseto cortico-steroids, particularly prednisone as noted here resulting in sustained improvement for this patient without significant side effects typically associated long-term steroid use or requiring additional immunosuppressive therapy seen with scleroderma management. This rapid and effective treatment outcome underscores a critical aspect of Shulman's syndrome. Here's how you can do it: ```swift let viewHeight = view.bounds.height let viewWidth = view.bounds.width let randomEdge = arc4random_uniform(4) // 4 edges, 0 to 3 var randomXPosition: CGFloat = 0 var randomYPosition: CGFloat = 0 switch randomEdge { case 0: // Top edge randomXPosition = CGFloat(arc4random_uniform(UInt32(viewWidth))) randomYPosition = 0 case 1: // Right edge randomXPosition = viewWidth randomYPosition = CGFloat(arc4random_uniform(UInt32(viewHeight))) case 2: // Bottom edge randomXPosition = CGFloat(arc4random_uniform(UInt32(viewWidth))) randomYPosition = viewHeight case 3: // Left edge randomXPosition = 0 randomYPosition = CGFloat(arc4random_uniform(UInt32(viewHeight))) default: // Should never happen randomXPosition = 0 randomYPosition = 0 } // randomXPosition and randomYPosition now contain random edge positions ``` ### Explanation - randomEdge: This randomizes which edge of the screen the position will be on (0 = top, 1 = right, 2 = bottom, 3 = left). - randomXPosition and randomYPosition: Depending on the edge, one of the positions (X or Y) will be 0 or the edge of the screen (viewWidth or viewHeight), and the other will be a random value within the screen's bounds. texts.full_text: This moderate to difficult point-to-point trail is 4.6 miles and if you want to day hike; it will take two cars, one parked at Deep Gap, the starting point, and another at Stanley Gap, where you end up after completing your trek through the beautiful North Carolina wilderness. The trail is known for its stunning panoramic views of several iconic peaks such as Hawksbill Rock, Shining Rock, and Blackstock Knob to name a few. It's also part of the larger Benton MacKenzie Trail network which spans over 10 miles in total if you decide to extend your adventure further into other trails connected or intersecting Stanley Gap trail. The hike itself is best attempted during spring through fall, as winter can bring severe weather conditions and potential closures due to snow accumulation on the higher elevation areas of this trail. The journey starts at Deep Gap where hikers will immediately notice a gradual incline that leads them into an enchanting forest landscape filled with diverse flora including rhododendrons which are particularly beautiful when they bloom during late spring, creating vibrant color contrasts against green foliage backdrop. texts.full_text: ucted by offsetting successive courses of stone (or brick) at the springline of the walls so that they project towards the archway's center from each supporting side, until the courses meet at the apex of the archway (often, the last gap is bridged with a flat stone). For a corbeled vault covering, the technique is extended in three dimensions along the lengths of two opposing walls. Although an improvement in load-bearing efficiency over the post and lintel design, corbeled arches are not entirely self-supporting structures, and the corbeled arch is sometimes termed a false arch for this reason. Unlike "true" arches, not all of the structure's tensile stresses caused by the weight of the superstructure are transformed into compressive stresses. CorbHere's a complete description of a corbel arch: --- A corbel arch is a type of arch formed by the technique of corbeling, where courses of masonry are offset or projected incrementally towards the center of the opening, creating a stepped, overhanging appearance. Corbels are typically used to span a void, such as a doorway, window, or bridge, and are also employed in corbel vaulting to support the roof of a building or other superstructure. Construction of a Corbels Arch: 1. Foundation Courses: The arch begins with the first few courses of masonry laid horizontally and evenly, serving as the base of the arch. 2. Initiation of Corbels: From a certain height, each successive course is laid with the blocks progressively projecting further into the archway, creating a slight overhang. This offset is typically the height of the masonry unit being used. 3. Continuation of Corbels: This pattern of offset courses continues until the opposing corbels meet at the apex

texts.full_text: below Michael Winner LONdON (AP) - "Death Wish" director Michael Winner, a British filmmaker, restaurant critic and bon vivant, died Monday. He was 77. Winner's wife, Geraldine, said he died at his London home after an illness. Winner's 30 movies included three "Death Wish" films starring the late Charles Bronson. Many of his features sit at the schlockier end of the spectrum, but he also worked with Hollywood icons including Marlon Brando, Burt Lancaster, Robert Mitchum and Faye Dunaway. One of his earliest films was the 1962 nudist feature "Some Like It Cool"; later, he specialized in thrillers and action movies, including "The Mechanic," ''Scorpio" and the violent "Death Wish" series. Winner never took criticism of his films too seriously. "If you want art, don't mess about with movies," he once said. "Buy a Picasso." born in London in 1935, Winner was writing a showbiz column for a local newspaper at age 14 and broke into movies in his early 20s. He was also a successful restaurant critic, writing a popular column for The Sunday Times of London. He also starred in the television series "Winner's Dinners," in which he traveled around the world sampling food. In 2006, Winner founded a campaign to promote the safety of police officers, which later became a charity called the Police Memorial Trust. He was knighted by Queen Elizabeth II in 2011 for his work with the trust. Winner is survived by his wife. ___ Editor's Note: This story has been corrected to show that Winner's column appeared in The Sunday Times of London, not The Time

Using the service locator in an extension method is considered a bad practice? How would you unit-test the extension method call? A: But when writing code, we often need to "inject" a service (injectable) into an entity (newable) This is not the case- if you find the need to do this then there is some functionality that exists in the Entity which should be in a service. Let's say your newable is ShoppingCart and your injectable is a database repository. You want to be able to do this: // somehow cart alreadY got the repository cart.save(); Well, you're doing it wrong. Instead you need to switch things around and do: respository.save( cart );

The user of conventional keyboards cannot type with both hands and hold the keyboard securely, there are often periods of transition going between hands where it is not held securely at all. In Portrait screen orientation, there is no good way for a standard keyboard to fill the screen with bigger keys if the keys are perpendicular and square with the bottom of the screen and the layout remains appearing like a known standard QWERTY keyboard. The problem is more evident on smaller devices, such as phones and pagers, where thumbs are often the primary vehicles of engaging the keys. Due to the smaller size of the keyboard on these devices, typically the typing finger covers a large percentage of the keyboard, obscuring many/most of the keys and the location of the keys during typing effort. Also, to the best knowledge of the Applicant, existing virtual type keyboards are provided in specific configurations and specific locations based on the coordinate system of the screen that they are disposed on. texts.full_text: Julian C. Kerbis Peterhans (jkerbis\@fieldmuseum.org), Steven M. Goodman (sgoodman\@fieldmuseum.org), Michael Bartonjo (abartonjo\@yahoo.com), Bruce D. Patterson (bpatterson\@fieldmuseum.org) 1. INTRODUCTION {#jzs12313-sec-0001} =============== The Paleotropical slit‐faced bats, family NycteriDae, all belong to the genus *Nycteris* with 13 of 16 recognized species found in continental Africa and offshore islands, one species on Madagascar, and two species endemic to south‐East Asia (Mammal Diversity Database, [2019](#jzs12313-bib-0021){ref-type="ref"}; Simmons, [2005](#jzs12313-bib-0034){ref-type="ref"}). Members of the Nycteridae are readily recognizable by their nose leaves, which are divided by a deep furrow running th...rogh the middle. This distinctive character is accompanied by long ears and large eyes (Kingdon et al., [2013](#jzs12313-bib-0019){ref-type="ref"}). The species of Nycteris, also known as slit-faced bats due to their characteristic facial morphology, are insectivorous bats that occur in a variety of habitats ranging from tropical rainforests to savannas and grasslands.
